sections.title: Domain & Infrastructure Footprinting — Domain & DNS Records

items.title: Collect WHOIS / RDAP registration data
items.description: Pull WHOIS/RDAP for the apex domain and related domains to identify registrant org, registrar, name servers, and creation/expiry dates. Useful for maturity tracking and audit evidence.
items.how_to_find: Registrant org, abuse/admin contacts, name servers, creation/expiry dates, and registrar — even if privacy-protected, the registrar and NS often leak the hosting relationship.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for collect whois / rdap registration data, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Enumerate DNS records (A/AAAA/MX/NS/TXT/CNAME)
items.description: Query the full record set to discover mail servers, third-party SaaS via CNAMEs, and SPF/DKIM/DMARC TXT records that reveal infrastructure providers. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Domain & Infrastructure Footprinting — Domain & DNS Records: MX hosts (mail provider), TXT/SPF includes (third-party senders), NS delegation, and CNAMEs pointing to external SaaS that widen the attack surface
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enumerate dns records (a/aaaa/mx/ns/txt/cname), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Perform reverse DNS (PTR) lookups
items.description: Resolve IPs back to hostnames across the target's ranges to discover internal naming conventions and additional hosts on shared netblocks. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: PTR records revealing hostnames, naming patterns (e.g. dev/stage/internal), and co-located hosts that may belong to the same org.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for perform reverse dns (ptr) lookups, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Review historical DNS / passive DNS
items.description: Use passive DNS and historical resolution datasets to find prior IPs, retired subdomains, and origin servers hidden behind a CDN/WAF. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Old A records that may expose the real origin behind Cloudflare/Akamai, decommissioned hosts still alive, and IP churn that maps hosting migrations.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for review historical dns / passive dns, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Passively enumerate subdomains from OSINT sources
items.description: Aggregate subdomains from passive sources (search engines, threat intel feeds, DNS aggregators) without sending traffic to the target. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Domain & Infrastructure Footprinting — Subdomain Enumeration: Aggregate subdomains from passive sources (search engines, threat intel feeds, DNS aggregators) without sending traffic to the target
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for passively enumerate subdomains from osint sources, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP WSTG - Enumerate Applications on Webserver

items.title: Mine certificate transparency logs
items.description: Query CT logs (crt.sh, Censys, Google CT) to discover subdomains issued TLS certificates, including internal and pre-release hostnames. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Subdomains in SAN fields, wildcard certs, and recently issued certs that hint at new infrastructure or staging environments.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for mine certificate transparency logs, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Generate subdomain permutations from known patterns
items.description: Derive likely additional subdomains by permuting/altering discovered names (e.g. Validate this during security reviews, under Domain & Infrastructure Footprinting — Subdomain Enumeration. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: New resolvable hosts from permutations; corroborate against passive DNS rather than brute-forcing the target directly.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for generate subdomain permutations from known patterns, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Flag dangling subdomains (takeover candidates)
items.description: Identify subdomains with CNAMEs pointing to unclaimed third-party services (deprovisioned S3, Heroku, GitHub Pages, etc.) as subdomain-takeover candidates. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: CNAMEs resolving to fingerprintable "not found" pages on SaaS providers; flag but do not claim/exploit during passive recon.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for flag dangling subdomains (takeover candidates), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Domain & Infrastructure Footprinting — IP Ranges, ASN & Exposed Services

items.title: Map ASN and owned IP ranges
items.description: Identify the Autonomous System Number(s) and netblocks registered to the org to scope owned IP space for the assessment. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: ASN ownership, CIDR ranges, and RIR allocations attributable to the target — distinguish owned ranges from shared cloud/CDN IPs.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for map asn and owned ip ranges, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Inventory exposed services via Shodan
items.description: Query Shodan across the target's IPs/orgs to enumerate open ports, banners, service versions, and exposed devices without touching the target. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exposed RDP/SSH/databases/ICS, outdated banners, default credentials hints, and certificates linking additional hosts to the org.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for inventory exposed services via shodan, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Cross-reference exposed hosts via Censys
items.description: Use Censys internet-wide scan data to corroborate exposed hosts, certificate associations, and service fingerprints found via Shodan. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Hosts, certificates and software versions in Censys; pivot on shared certs/keys to discover additional target-owned infrastructure.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for cross-reference exposed hosts via censys, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify CDN/WAF and hunt for origin IPs
items.description: Determine whether hosts sit behind a CDN/WAF and look for origin-IP leaks via historical DNS, SSL cert reuse, and scan-data pivots. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Origin IPs exposed in old DNS or matching certificates that bypass the CDN/WAF; note these as protection gaps without active probing.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify cdn/waf and hunt for origin ips, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Domain & Infrastructure Footprinting — Technology Stack Fingerprinting

items.title: Fingerprint web technologies from public responses
items.description: Identify CMS, frameworks, web servers, JS libraries, and analytics from response headers, HTML, and public technology-detection datasets. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Server/framework names and versions, CMS plugins/themes, and outdated components that map to known CVEs.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for fingerprint web technologies from public responses, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: OWASP WSTG - Fingerprint Web Application Framework

items.title: Inventory third-party SaaS and dependencies
items.description: Enumerate third-party services (payment, auth, support, marketing, CDNs) referenced in public assets to map supply-chain exposure. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: External script origins, OAuth providers, ticketing/support widgets, and SaaS subdomains that expand the trust boundary.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for inventory third-party saas and dependencies, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Determine hosting and cloud provider footprint
items.description: Attribute hosts to AWS/Azure/GCP/on-prem via IP ranges, certificates, and CNAME patterns to inform cloud-specific recon. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Which provider(s) host production vs staging, regional spread, and provider-specific service names that reveal architecture.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for determine hosting and cloud provider footprint, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Correlate detected versions to known CVEs
items.description: Map fingerprinted component versions to public vulnerability databases to prioritize likely-vulnerable surfaces — passively, from disclosed data. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Outdated/EOL components with public exploits; record candidate CVEs for later authorized active validation, not exploitation during recon.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for correlate detected versions to known cves, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Enumerate employees from LinkedIn and professional sites
items.description: Build a roster of current employees, titles, and departments from LinkedIn and similar public professional networks. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Names, roles (especially IT/security/admin/finance), tenure, and technologies named in profiles that reveal the internal stack.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enumerate employees from linkedin and professional sites, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Map organizational structure and key personnel
items.description: Reconstruct reporting lines and identify high-value targets (executives, sysadmins, helpdesk) useful for social-engineering scenarios. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Decision-makers, IT/security staff, and helpdesk/support roles likely to be impersonated or targeted in phishing.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for map organizational structure and key personnel, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify technical staff and disclosed tooling
items.description: Find engineers/DevOps/security staff whose public profiles, talks, or job posts disclose internal technologies, vendors, and processes. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Stack details from resumes/job listings (languages, cloud, security tools), conference talks, and OSS contributions tied to the org.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify technical staff and disclosed tooling, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Compile a candidate phishing/target list
items.description: Consolidate employee data (name, role, email, location) into a prioritized list of likely social-engineering targets for the engagement scope. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: High-value targets with verified emails and roles; ensure list stays within authorized engagement scope and legal boundaries.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for compile a candidate phishing/target list, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: People & Organization — Email Pattern & Contact Harvesting

items.title: Derive the corporate email naming convention
items.description: Determine the email format (first.last, flast, etc.) from harvested addresses so any employee name can be converted to a probable address. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: People & Organization — Email Pattern & Contact Harvesting: Confirmed pattern across multiple known addresses, plus any exceptions for shared/role accounts (info@, hr@, security@)
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for derive the corporate email naming convention, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Harvest published email addresses
items.description: Collect email addresses exposed in search engines, websites, PGP keyservers, and document metadata to seed the address list. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Valid addresses with sources, role accounts, and any personal addresses tied to staff that broaden the contact map.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for harvest published email addresses, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Validate addresses against breach/presence data
items.description: Check harvested addresses for existence on services and breach corpora (without active SMTP probing of the target) to confirm live accounts. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: People & Organization — Email Pattern & Contact Harvesting: Which addresses are confirmed real and registered to external services — useful both for targeting and breach-exposure correlation
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for validate addresses against breach/presence data, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Assess email spoofing posture (SPF/DKIM/DMARC)
items.description: Review published SPF, DKIM, and DMARC records to gauge how easily the domain can be spoofed in phishing — all from public DNS. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Missing/weak DMARC (p=none), permissive SPF (~all), and absent DKIM that make spoofing trivial against the org's users.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for assess email spoofing posture (spf/dkim/dmarc), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Enumerate employee and org social accounts
items.description: Discover official and personal social-media accounts (X, Facebook, Instagram, GitHub, etc.) tied to the org and its staff. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Account handles, cross-platform username reuse, and links between personal and work identities that aid pretexting.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enumerate employee and org social accounts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Extract metadata from posted media
items.description: Analyze publicly posted photos/files for EXIF/geolocation and background details that leak physical locations, badges, or screens. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: GPS coordinates in EXIF, visible badges/whiteboards/monitors in office photos, and device/software hints in metadata.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for extract metadata from posted media, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Build behavioral and interest profiles
items.description: Profile interests, schedules, and relationships from public posts to craft credible social-engineering pretexts within scope. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Routines (travel, events), interests, and trust relationships usable for pretexting — kept strictly within engagement rules.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for build behavioral and interest profiles, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Hunt for sensitive disclosures in posts
items.description: Search public posts for inadvertent disclosures — screenshots with credentials, internal URLs, system names, or project details. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Leaked internal hostnames, tooling, credentials in screenshots, or project codenames that expand intel on the environment.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for hunt for sensitive disclosures in posts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Credential & Breach Exposure — Breach Corpora & Paste Sites

items.title: Search breach corpora for target email/domain hits
items.description: Query breach-aggregation services for accounts at the target domain that appear in known data breaches. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Which corporate accounts appear in breaches, the breach sources/dates, and whether plaintext or hashed passwords are included.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for search breach corpora for target email/domain hits, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify plaintext or cracked passwords in leaks
items.description: Determine whether leaked records expose usable plaintext (or already-cracked) passwords for target accounts. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Plaintext/cracked passwords mapped to corporate accounts, and any password reuse patterns visible across multiple leaks.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify plaintext or cracked passwords in leaks, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Monitor paste sites for leaked target data
items.description: Search paste sites (Pastebin and mirrors) for dumps containing target emails, credentials, configs, or internal data. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Pasted credential dumps, config/secret leaks, and internal documents referencing the target, with capture of source and timestamp.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for monitor paste sites for leaked target data, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Catalog exposed PII and secondary identifiers
items.description: Record exposed PII (phones, addresses, security-question answers) that supports account recovery abuse or deeper targeting. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: PII usable for password resets / account recovery and identity verification bypass; treat as sensitive and scope-restricted.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for catalog exposed pii and secondary identifiers, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Assess relevance of exposed creds to live login surfaces
items.description: Map breached credentials to the target's identified login portals (VPN, webmail, SSO) to gauge credential-stuffing risk. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Credential & Breach Exposure — Credential-Stuffing Intelligence: Whether breached corporate emails align with discovered login endpoints; document candidate surfaces without active testing
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for assess relevance of exposed creds to live login surfaces, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify password-reuse and reset patterns
items.description: Analyze leaked passwords for reuse across services and predictable patterns (season+year, company name) informing spray candidates. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Reuse across breaches and obvious password patterns; build a prioritized, scope-bound candidate list — never spray during passive recon.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify password-reuse and reset patterns, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Determine MFA coverage on exposed accounts
items.description: From public/breach signals, infer which login surfaces enforce MFA so the team understands residual risk of exposed credentials. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Login portals lacking enforced MFA where exposed credentials would be directly usable; record as a control gap.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for determine mfa coverage on exposed accounts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check stealer-log / infostealer exposure
items.description: Search for target accounts appearing in infostealer log dumps, which often include fresh, valid session and credential data. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Presence in stealer logs (cookies, tokens, plaintext creds) indicating active compromise risk far beyond historical breaches.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check stealer-log / infostealer exposure, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Code & Document Leakage — Public Code & Secret Leakage

items.title: Dork public repos for the org and its assets
items.description: Search GitHub/GitLab for repositories, gists, and files referencing the target's domains, internal hostnames, or product names. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Public/forked repos by employees, gists, and code referencing internal systems that expose architecture or configuration.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for dork public repos for the org and its assets, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Scan discovered repos for hardcoded secrets
items.description: Run secret scanners over public repos/history to find API keys, tokens, passwords, and private keys committed by mistake. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Code & Document Leakage — Public Code & Secret Leakage: Run secret scanners over public repos/history to find API keys, tokens, passwords, and private keys committed by mistake
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for scan discovered repos for hardcoded secrets, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Inspect commit history and deleted content
items.description: Review full git history (not just HEAD) for secrets that were "removed" in later commits but remain recoverable from history. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Secrets present in historical commits/branches/tags even when scrubbed from the current tree; rotate-status of any keys found.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for inspect commit history and deleted content, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: GitHub - Removing sensitive data from a repository

items.title: Find leaked config and CI/CD secrets
items.description: Look for exposed config files, environment files, and CI/CD pipeline definitions that disclose endpoints, hosts, and embedded secrets. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Committed .env, config, or CI files with internal URLs, service accounts, and pipeline tokens usable to pivot into the environment.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for find leaked config and ci/cd secrets, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Code & Document Leakage — Document Metadata & Exposed Files

items.title: Discover public documents tied to the target
items.description: Find publicly hosted documents (PDF, DOCX, XLSX, PPTX) indexed in search engines or linked from the org's sites. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Inventory of public files, especially internal-looking documents (HR, finance, technical) that should not be exposed.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for discover public documents tied to the target, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Extract metadata (authors, software, paths)
items.description: Parse document metadata for usernames, internal file paths, software versions, printer names, and timestamps. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Internal usernames (for email/AD format), software versions (CVE mapping), UNC/file paths, and device names leaking infrastructure.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for extract metadata (authors, software, paths), apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify inadvertently exposed sensitive files
items.description: Flag exposed backups, spreadsheets, and exports containing credentials, PII, or internal data that should not be public. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exposed backups/exports with secrets or PII, default-indexed directories, and documents marked internal/confidential.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify inadvertently exposed sensitive files, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Build a username map from document authorship
items.description: Aggregate authorship/creator metadata across documents to derive the internal username convention and active-user list. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Code & Document Leakage — Document Metadata & Exposed Files: Consistent username format and an enumerated user list that supports email-format derivation and later authorized auth testing
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for build a username map from document authorship, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Dork for exposed files and directory listings
items.description: Use operators (site:, filetype:, intitle:index.of) to surface indexed sensitive files and open directory listings. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Indexed config/backup/log files, open directory listings, and sensitive filetypes that should not be publicly searchable.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for dork for exposed files and directory listings, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Locate exposed login panels and admin interfaces
items.description: Dork for indexed admin/login/console pages (inurl:admin, intitle:login) that reveal entry points to the environment. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Indexed admin/login portals, device management UIs, and default panels that broaden the authenticated attack surface.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for locate exposed login panels and admin interfaces, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Find error messages and info disclosure via dorks
items.description: Search for indexed pages leaking stack traces, SQL errors, version strings, or debug output that disclose internals. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Indexed error pages exposing software versions, paths, queries, or secrets that aid targeting and CVE mapping.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for find error messages and info disclosure via dorks, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Mine web archives and cached pages
items.description: Use the Wayback Machine and search-engine caches to recover removed pages, old endpoints, and previously exposed data. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Archived endpoints/parameters, removed sensitive pages, and historical content that still informs the current attack surface.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for mine web archives and cached pages, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Exposed Assets & Cloud — Public Cloud Storage Discovery

items.title: Enumerate candidate cloud storage buckets
items.description: Derive likely bucket/container names from the org name, domains, and products, then check public listing/read status. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exposed Assets & Cloud — Public Cloud Storage Discovery: Derive likely bucket/container names from the org name, domains, and products, then check public listing/read status
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for enumerate candidate cloud storage buckets, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify publicly listable / readable buckets
items.description: Determine which discovered buckets allow anonymous listing or object read, exposing the data inside. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Buckets with public ListBucket/GetObject; capture an inventory of exposed objects without downloading sensitive data wholesale.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify publicly listable / readable buckets, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Check Azure Blob and GCS exposure
items.description: Extend bucket discovery to Azure Blob containers and Google Cloud Storage to cover multi-cloud footprints. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Public Azure containers and GCS buckets tied to the org, including those referenced indirectly by CNAMEs/static sites.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for check azure blob and gcs exposure, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: Microsoft - Anonymous read access to blobs

items.title: Triage exposed object contents for sensitivity
items.description: Assess the nature of exposed objects (backups, source, PII, secrets) to gauge real impact of the misconfiguration. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Presence of secrets/PII/backups/source in exposed storage; document object types and access level without exfiltrating in bulk.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for triage exposed object contents for sensitivity, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Exposed Assets & Cloud — Exposed Dashboards & Dev/Staging

items.title: Find exposed dashboards and management consoles
items.description: Use scan-data search to find exposed admin dashboards (Kibana, Grafana, Jenkins, databases, Kubernetes) reachable from the internet. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Internet-exposed consoles, their auth state (open vs login), and versions; flag any unauthenticated dashboards as high risk.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for find exposed dashboards and management consoles, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Discover dev/staging/test environments
items.description: Identify non-production environments exposed publicly (dev., staging., uat., test.) that often have weaker controls. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exposed Assets & Cloud — Exposed Dashboards & Dev/Staging: Identify non-production environments exposed publicly (dev., staging., uat., test.) that often have weaker controls
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for discover dev/staging/test environments, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Detect exposed databases and data stores
items.description: Search scan data for internet-facing databases and caches (MongoDB, Elasticsearch, Redis, etc.) bound to public interfaces. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Publicly reachable data stores, authentication state, and version/banner data indicating potentially open access.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for detect exposed databases and data stores, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Identify exposed IoT/OT and remote-access services
items.description: Look for exposed remote-access (RDP, VNC, SSH) and IoT/OT/industrial devices attributable to the org via scan datasets. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Exposed RDP/VNC/SSH and IoT/ICS endpoints, their auth posture, and any default-credential or known-CVE indicators in banners.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for identify exposed iot/ot and remote-access services, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

sections.title: Exposed Assets & Cloud — Continuous Exposure Monitoring

items.title: Establish continuous attack-surface monitoring
items.description: Set up recurring discovery of new subdomains, IPs, and services so the external footprint stays current over the engagement. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: New assets appearing over time, change-over-baseline alerts, and coverage across domains/clouds to avoid blind spots.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for establish continuous attack-surface monitoring, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Monitor certificate transparency for new hosts
items.description: Subscribe to CT-log alerts for the target's domains to catch newly issued certificates revealing fresh infrastructure. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: New SAN entries and wildcard issuances signaling new subdomains/environments shortly after they are created.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for monitor certificate transparency for new hosts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

items.title: Set up breach and credential-exposure alerts
items.description: Configure alerting on the target's domains/emails so newly published breach or paste data is flagged promptly. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Notifications on new breach hits, paste-site leaks, and stealer-log appearances for monitored domains and key accounts.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for set up breach and credential-exposure alerts, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.

references.title: Have I Been Pwned - Domain search / notifications

items.title: Monitor public code hosts for new secret leaks
items.description: Continuously watch GitHub/GitLab for new commits/repos referencing the org that may leak secrets or sensitive code. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Newly committed secrets or sensitive references to the org as they are pushed, enabling fast detection and key rotation.
items.how_to_fix: Assign an owner for monitor public code hosts for new secret leaks, apply vendor or CIS hardening guidance, validate in staging, then re-run the same test that exposed the gap.
